name: Goldilocks NTT — Stage Specialization + Canal Dinámico + ILP

phases.name: Twiddle optimization + cleanup
phases.description: Stage specialization for power-of-2 twiddles (~40% of butterflies skip multiply) + fix semanticMatchStep dead code bug.

nodes.name: TA: Power-of-2 twiddle stage specialization in codegen

study.notes: Pre-compute isPow2Twiddle(omega, pair, stage, p) at Lean codegen time. In lowerStageVerified, for pow2 pairs emit shift instead of multiply. Table is already CT standard (0 mismatches). ~25 LOC. 39.71% of butterflies skip-able.

nodes.name: T12: Fix semanticMatchStep dead code bug

study.notes: Line 358: bypass semanticMatchStep (getVal := fun id => id is nonsensical). Replace with let s'' := s'. 2 LOC.

blocks.name: Twiddle optimization + cleanup
blocks.gate: lake build + benchmark.py --validation-only --fields goldilocks,babybear --sizes 14 + performance benchmark pre/post TA

phases.name: NTT Trick (investigación)
phases.description: Investigate NTT_64 × NTT_{N/64} decomposition where NTT_64 uses omega_64=8 (all shifts). Requires TB-1 viability gate.

nodes.name: TB-1: NTT trick viability study (READ ONLY)

study.notes: READ ONLY. Answer: does NTTStrategy need new constructor? Does NTTStage need per-stage root? Can twiddle table have 2 sections? Does Python reference match with decomposition?
Only if TB-1 = go. Add .twoStepGoldilocks to NTTStrategy. Generate 2-section twiddle table. PRECAUTION: decomposition changes operation order, output MUST match standard NTT.
Edge cases: N=4..2^20, 5 input types. 100% match vs standard NTT. ZERO tolerance.

blocks.name: NTT trick viability
blocks.gate: 1-page document: viable? constructor needed? per-stage root needed?

blocks.name: NTT trick validation
blocks.gate: test_ntt_trick.py 100% PASS for all sizes × inputs

phases.name: Canal dinámico + benchmark baseline
phases.description: Connect e-graph dynamic cost to plan selection. Measure gap vs Plonky3.

study.notes: ~12 LOC across 2 files. lazy_eq_solinas_cost uses reductionCostForHW directly (NOT Plan.totalCost) — safe. native_decide tests are relative comparisons — safe.
useDynamicCost is currently 100% dead code (declared line 120, never read). Wire to selectPlanWith using costFn dispatch. ~10 LOC.
Measure gap post-TA. Reference: v3.9.0 gap was 1.5-1.8x. If post-TA gap <10% → skip D. If >10% → D is needed.

blocks.name: Canal dinámico + benchmark
blocks.gate: useDynamicCost=false identical to v3.9.0 + gap table vs Plonky3

phases.name: ILP explícito (PROBABLE)
phases.description: Interleave 2 mul+umulh pairs before REDC to hide 3-cycle latency. For the 60% of butterflies that still need full multiply.

study.notes: ~40 LOC new function + ~5 LOC dispatch + ~3 LOC candidate. NTTStage.ilpFactor already exists (NTTPlan.lean:73). Edge: halfSize<2 fallback, halfSize odd last pair. NOT modify lowerDIFButterflyByReduction.

blocks.name: ILP explícito
blocks.gate: ILP2 = ILP1 numerically + ILP2 >= ILP1 performance + gap table vs Plonky3

correctness: Goldilocks NTT output matches Python reference for all sizes (2..2^20)
ILP2 produces numerically identical output to ILP1
useDynamicCost=false produces identical code to v3.9.0
performance: TA: ~25-30% Goldilocks speedup (40% butterflies skip multiply)
TD: ILP2 >= ILP1 for all sizes

properties.description: isPow2Twiddle correctly identifies power-of-2 twiddles for all stages
Specialized butterfly (shift) produces same output as general (multiply)
BabyBear NTT unchanged (no pow2 twiddles in BabyBear)
useDynamicCost=false produces identical plan selection to v3.9.0
ILP2 output = ILP1 output for all N and inputs
ILP2 performance >= ILP1 for all sizes
